approved.description: true ONLY when every finding is resolved — all your feedback addressed at any severity (minor and nit included), not just blockers.

summary.description: One-paragraph assessment of the change as it currently stands.

id.description: Stable identifier reused across rounds for the same issue, e.g. F1, F2.

location.description: file:line (or file) the finding applies to.

issue.description: What is wrong and why it matters.

status.description: resolved once the authoring engineer has adequately addressed it (by a fix or a dispute you accept); otherwise open.

concession.description: When you mark a finding resolved by ACCEPTING the author's dispute (no code change), the specific argument or code (file:line) that convinced you. Resolving a disputed finding without a stated reason is capitulation — hold it open instead. Empty string when the finding was resolved by a fix, or is still open.

responseToRebuttal.description: Directly address the authoring engineer's disputes from the previous round: concede (and mark the finding resolved) or hold firm with specific reasoning. Empty string on the first round.
